_documentation.title: Database JSON Schemas — Full API Representation
_documentation.description: JSON examples for each entity in the schema, showing how SQL tables serialize to API responses. Includes flat forms, expanded/nested forms, and one complete complex transaction object.

conventions.primary_keys: Kept as integers in API responses. Use user_id / transaction_id etc. as field names matching SQL columns.
conventions.foreign_keys: In flat (list) responses, FKs are returned as raw integers (e.g. sender_id: 1). In expanded (detail) responses, the FK field is replaced by a full nested object (e.g. sender: { ... }).
conventions.decimals: DECIMAL SQL columns serialize as JSON numbers with 2 decimal places. A parallel *_cents integer field is also included to avoid floating-point drift in client calculations.
conventions.datetimes: All DATETIME / TIMESTAMP columns serialize as ISO 8601 strings in UTC (e.g. '2024-03-10T19:30:00Z').
conventions.enums: ENUM-style columns (status, user_type, role) serialize as lowercase strings.
conventions.sensitive_fields: account_number is masked (last four digits only) in API responses. raw_sms_body and raw_transaction_ref are omitted from standard responses and only available to internal/admin endpoints.
conventions.computed_fields: Fields marked 'computed' have no direct SQL column — they are derived via aggregation (COUNT, SUM) at query time.
conventions.hateoas_links: Every entity object includes a _links block with hypermedia navigation URLs, following REST best practices.

sql_to_json_mapping._note: Column-by-column mapping for every table. Shows SQL type → JSON type, any transformation applied, and whether the field appears in list vs detail responses.

USERS.notes: MASKED — only last 4 digits returned. Full value never serialized.
SUM(amount) WHERE sender_id = user_id. Detail endpoint only.
SUM(amount) WHERE receiver_id = user_id. Detail endpoint only.

TRANSACTIONS.notes: Flat: integer. Expanded: full User object.
Same FK expansion pattern as sender.
2 decimal places. Always positive — direction is inferred from sender/receiver.
amount × 100 as integer. Avoids float drift in client.
Sender's balance immediately after this transaction.
OMITTED from standard responses. Internal/admin only.
Array from TRANSACTION_PARTICIPANTS. Detail endpoint only.

TRANSACTION_CATEGORIES.notes: COUNT(*) of transactions in this category.

TRANSACTION_PARTICIPANTS.notes: FK → TRANSACTIONS. Not repeated when nested inside a transaction object.
FK → USERS. Expanded to user object in detail responses.

SYSTEM_LOGS.notes: When the system processed this log entry.
OMITTED from standard API. Sensitive raw SMS text.

user._description: A single user record. account_number is masked. Returned by GET /api/v1/users/:id
user._sql_source: SELECT u.*, COUNT(t_sent.transaction_id) AS transactions_sent FROM USERS u LEFT JOIN TRANSACTIONS t_sent ON t_sent.sender_id = u.user_id WHERE u.user_id = 1

stats._note: Computed fields — aggregated at query time, not stored columns

transaction_category._description: A transaction category record. Returned by GET /api/v1/categories/:id
transaction_category._sql_source: SELECT c.*, COUNT(t.transaction_id) AS transaction_count FROM TRANSACTION_CATEGORIES c LEFT JOIN TRANSACTIONS t ON t.category_id = c.category_id WHERE c.category_id = 3
transaction_category.description: Person-to-person transfers via mobile money platforms such as MTN MoMo and Airtel Money.

transaction_flat._description: Flat (compact) transaction — used in list responses. FK fields remain as integer IDs. Returned by GET /api/v1/transactions
transaction_flat._sql_source: SELECT * FROM TRANSACTIONS ORDER BY transaction_date DESC LIMIT 20

transaction_participant._description: A single participant record for a transaction. Returned by GET /api/v1/transactions/:id/participants
transaction_participant._sql_source: SELECT p.*, u.full_name, u.phone_number FROM TRANSACTION_PARTICIPANTS p JOIN USERS u ON u.user_id = p.user_id WHERE p.transaction_id = 1001

user._note: FK user_id expanded to a minimal user object in this context

system_log._description: A system log entry linked to a transaction. raw_sms_body is omitted. Returned by GET /api/v1/transactions/:id/logs
system_log._sql_source: SELECT log_id, transaction_id, sms_date, processed_at, processing_status FROM SYSTEM_LOGS WHERE transaction_id = 1001

complex_transaction_object._description: The complete expanded transaction object — all related data nested inline. Returned by GET /api/v1/transactions/1001?expand=sender,receiver,category,participants,logs. This is what you would cache client-side or display on a transaction detail screen.
complex_transaction_object._sql_equivalent: SELECT t.*, u_s.full_name AS sender_name, u_s.phone_number AS sender_phone, u_r.full_name AS receiver_name, u_r.phone_number AS receiver_phone, c.category_name, c.description AS category_desc FROM TRANSACTIONS t JOIN USERS u_s ON u_s.user_id = t.sender_id JOIN USERS u_r ON u_r.user_id = t.receiver_id JOIN TRANSACTION_CATEGORIES c ON c.category_id = t.category_id WHERE t.transaction_id = 1001 -- Then separate queries for participants and logs

sender._note: FK sender_id → USERS. Full user object embedded. account_number masked.

receiver._note: FK receiver_id → USERS. Separate user object — same table, different role.

category._note: FK category_id → TRANSACTION_CATEGORIES. Full category object embedded.
category.description: Person-to-person transfers via mobile money platforms such as MTN MoMo and Airtel Money.

participants._note: 1:N from TRANSACTION_PARTICIPANTS. The pivot table disappears — data collapses into an array. Each entry nests the related USERS record.
participants._sql_source: SELECT p.participant_id, p.role, u.user_id, u.full_name, u.phone_number, u.user_type FROM TRANSACTION_PARTICIPANTS p JOIN USERS u ON u.user_id = p.user_id WHERE p.transaction_id = 1001

system_log._note: 1:1 or 1:N from SYSTEM_LOGS. raw_sms_body is OMITTED here — present in DB but never exposed in standard API responses.
system_log._sql_source: SELECT log_id, sms_date, processed_at, processing_status FROM SYSTEM_LOGS WHERE transaction_id = 1001

api_response_envelopes._description: Real API responses always wrap entity objects in an envelope with metadata. These show what the full HTTP response body looks like.

error.message: Transaction with ID 9999 does not exist.
